official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-go api/datadogV1/api_monitors.go (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: exact operations: GET /api/v1/monitor with query params group_states, name, tags, monitor_tags, with_downtimes, id_offset, page, page_size; PUT /api/v1/monitor/{monitor_id}; auth header names DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY; the official ListMonitorsWithPagination helper advances the page parameter with a default page_size of 100 until a page returns fewer results than page_size

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-go api/datadogV1/model_monitor.go (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: monitor object fields and JSON tags; readonly fields (id, created, creator, modified, overall_state, matching_downtimes); restricted_roles as the list of role identifiers allowed to edit the monitor; priority 1 (high) to 5 (low)

official_sources.title: datadog-api-client-go api/datadogV1/model_monitor_update_request.go (official Datadog client source)
official_sources.used_for: MonitorUpdateRequest: every field optional (partial update body), including options, restricted_roles (nullable list), priority (nullable int), tags, query, message

official_sources.title: How to set up RBAC for Monitors — Datadog Docs
official_sources.used_for: restricted_roles semantics on create/edit: role UUIDs restrict who can edit; setting restricted_roles to null allows editing for all users with Monitor Write permission — so an update must never emit null accidentally

official_sources.title: Rate Limits — Datadog API Reference
official_sources.used_for: X-RateLimit-Limit/Period/Remaining/Reset/Name response headers, their meanings, and 429 retry guidance

official_sources.title: Monitors — Datadog API Reference
official_sources.used_for: current operation inventory: monitor CRUD and pagination live under /api/v1/monitor; the v2 monitor namespace covers only configuration policies, notification rules, and monitor templates

research.language_rationale: The fleet tooling this reconciler joins is Go. Datadog's official datadog-api-client-go is a generated wrapper over these same v1 endpoints, and the verify environment is offline and stdlib-only, so net/http against the documented v1 monitor operations is the credible integration surface; the client source doubles as the authoritative wire reference.

verified_facts: Monitor list/edit are v1 operations: GET /api/v1/monitor and PUT /api/v1/monitor/{monitor_id}; the v2 monitor namespace holds only configuration policies, notification rules, and templates.
Management endpoints authenticate with both DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY request headers.

GET /api/v1/monitor pages with page (0-based) and page_size; the official client's pager uses page_size 100 by default and stops when a page returns fewer results than page_size; monitor_tags is a comma-joined filter on the monitor's own tags.
The list response is a bare JSON array of monitor objects.
MonitorUpdateRequest fields are all optional; readonly monitor fields include id, created, creator, modified, overall_state, and matching_downtimes.

restricted_roles is a list of role UUIDs allowed to edit the monitor; null means every user with Monitor Write can edit, so updates must omit the key rather than send null when preserving an unrestricted monitor.
priority is a nullable integer from 1 (high) to 5 (low).
Rate limiting is reported through X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Period, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, and X-RateLimit-Name headers plus 429 responses with an {"errors": [...]} envelope.
